title: Quick code tutorial: List models
text:
Quick code tutorial: List models Last Updated : 2025-11-08 You can list supported foundation models programmatically with the watsonx.ai API and SDK. This tutorial demonstrates how to list the supported foundation models and find model IDs. Work with Python or Curl. You can complete this tutorial in less than 5 minutes. Detailed information Supported models for inferencing Embeddings models Reranker models Multimodal models Time series forecasting models Models for chat, tool-calling, and RAG Prerequisite Complete the one-time steps to gather your credentials and other information and prepare your system. See Prerequisite one-time tasks . List models Python Curl To list all models, add the following code to the example.py file. Replace {watsonx_ai_url} and {apikey} with your values. from ibm_watsonx_ai import APIClient from ibm_watsonx_ai import Credentials

credentials = Credentials(
               url = "{watsonx_ai_url}" ,
               api_key = {apikey},
                  )

api_client = APIClient(credentials)

api_client.foundation_models.TextModels.show() Copy to clipboard When you find the model that you want, copy the model ID. To list models, run the following curl request. Replace {token} and {watsonx_ai_url} with your values. curl -X GET                             \
-H "Authorization: Bearer {token}" \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-H "Accept: application/json" \
{watsonx_ai_url}/ml/v1/foundation_model_specs?version=2024-05-31 Copy to clipboard When you find the model that you want, copy the model ID. Next steps Understand how models can address your use case, including information on model modalities, supported languages, tuning, and indemnification. See Choosing a model . Rerun a tutorial with a new model ID. Try another quick start coding tutorial. Learn more Python model specs watsonx.ai REST API Supported models
